title: Today's AI Brief: Hands-On With Gemini Spark
summary: Google's Gemini Spark AI agent and Groq's $650M funding round make headlines
imageAlt: Image from source article for Hands-On With Gemini Spark: I Gave It Access to My Life and It Friend-Zoned My Boyfriend
imageCaption: Hands-On With Gemini Spark: I Gave It Access to My Life and It Friend-Zoned My Boyfriend
imageSourceTitle: Hands-On With Gemini Spark: I Gave It Access to My Life and It Friend-Zoned My Boyfriend

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI briefing. Google recently introduced Gemini Spark, an always-on AI agent that connects to your personal data, completes online tasks, and automates daily interactions. It's like having a super-smart assistant that's always on. I have to admit, I'm both excited and a little uneasy about this level of automation in my life.

In a hands-on test, Wired gave Gemini Spark access to their life, and it apparently friend-zoned their boyfriend. I guess that's one way to test the boundaries of AI. The idea is to see how this agent can manage your daily tasks and interactions, but it's clear there are some kinks to work out – like not turning your romantic life into a platonic one. It's a funny example of how AI can be a bit clueless about human relationships.

Meanwhile, AI chip startup Groq is reportedly raising $650M in funding. This comes after Nvidia's $20B acquisition of another AI chip company that wasn't quite an acquisition. It's a lot of money, and it shows investors are betting big on the future of AI hardware. I'm curious to see how this investment will accelerate the development of more powerful AI systems.
Another Player in the AI Chip Market

Hands-On With Gemini Spark: I Gave It Access to My Life and It Friend-Zoned My Boyfriend is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: At its recent I/O developer conference, Google introduced Gemini Spark as an always-on agent that connects to your personal data, completes online tasks, and automates aspects of your daily interactions. It’s Google’s take on the viral OpenClaw agent that rocked Silicon Valley at the start of 2026. OpenClaw’s early adopters handed their entire lives over to... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

After Nvidia’s $20B not-aqui-hire, AI chip startup Groq reportedly raising $650M is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: In Brief Posted: 10:27 AM PDT · May 29, 2026 Image Credits: Nuthawut Somsuk / Getty Images Dominic-Madori Davis After Nvidia’s $20B not-aqui-hire, AI chip startup Groq reportedly raising $650M Groq is looking to raise $650 million in new funding from existing investors, sources tell Axios , as it leans into its inference neocloud business that relies on its... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
